title: UN R156 7.1.4 — Software update recording and reporting: update audit trail completeness
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=UN R156, clause=7.1.4, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for 7.1.4.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that UN R156 7.1.4 (Recording and reporting of software updates) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.43.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.43' running on catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention).
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: OTA update audit logs, software version registries, type-approval documentation databases, regulatory reporting systems.
spl:
index=ota_updates sourcetype IN (ota_audit_log, sw_version_registry) earliest=-90d
| eval record_complete=if(isnotnull(rxswin) AND isnotnull(sw_version) AND isnotnull(update_date) AND isnotnull(approval_authority), 1, 0)
| eval reported_to_authority=if(isnotnull(authority_notification_date), 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_updates sum(record_complete) AS complete_records sum(reported_to_authority) AS reported dc(vehicle_type) AS types_covered BY vehicle_type, reporting_period
| eval record_completeness_pct=round(100*complete_records/total_updates, 1)
| eval reporting_pct=round(100*reported/total_updates, 1)
| where record_completeness_pct<100 OR reporting_pct<100
| table vehicle_type, reporting_period, total_updates, complete_records, record_completeness_pct, reported, reporting_pct, types_covered
description: Monitors software update audit trail completeness and regulatory reporting obligations per UN R156 7.1.4, ensuring all updates are properly recorded with RXSWIN identifiers and reported to approval authorities.
value: UN R156 requires recording and reporting of all software updates including RXSWIN (Regulation Software Identification Number). Audit trail completeness monitoring ensures regulatory reporting obligations are met.
implementation: (1) Ingest OTA audit logs with rxswin, sw_version, update_date, approval_authority; (2) schedule monthly; (3) flag incomplete records for compliance team. Indexes required: `index=ota_updates`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ota_audit_log`, `sourcetype=sw_version_registry`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `complete_records`, `record_complete`, `record_completeness_pct`, `reported`, `reported_to_authority`, `reporting_pct`.
knownFalsePositives: Tier-2 framework drift during quarterly reviews, scheduled crosswalk updates, or controlled exception cycles.
grandmaExplanation: We watch that every software update is recorded and reported the way the type-approval rules expect.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure data sources: OTA update audit logs, software version registries, type-approval documentation databases, regulatory reporting systems.
• See docs/implementation-guide.md for app layout and inputs.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest OTA audit logs with rxswin, sw_version, update_date, approval_authority; (2) schedule monthly; (3) flag incomplete records for compliance team. Indexes required: `index=ota_updates`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ota_audit_log`, `sourcetype=sw_version_registry`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL (save as report or alert; tune time range and thresholds):

```spl
index=ota_updates sourcetype IN (ota_audit_log, sw_version_registry) earliest=-90d
| eval record_complete=if(isnotnull(rxswin) AND isnotnull(sw_version) AND isnotnull(update_date) AND isnotnull(approval_authority), 1, 0)
| eval reported_to_authority=if(isnotnull(authority_notification_date), 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_updates sum(record_complete) AS complete_records sum(reported_to_authority) AS reported dc(vehicle_type) AS types_covered BY vehicle_type, reporting_period
| eval record_completeness_pct=round(100*complete_records/total_updates, 1)
| eval reporting_pct=round(100*reported/total_updates, 1)
| where record_completeness_pct<100 OR reporting_pct<100
| table vehicle_type, reporting_period, total_updates, complete_records, record_completeness_pct, reported, reporting_pct, types_covered
```

Understanding this SPL

**UN R156 7.1.4 — Software update recording and reporting: update audit trail completeness** — UN R156 requires recording and reporting of all software updates including RXSWIN (Regulation Software Identification Number). Audit trail completeness monitoring ensures regulatory reporting obligations are met.

Output fields and breakdowns should match the panel fields listed in **Visualization**.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with the crosswalk for the tier-2 framework clause, so monitoring evidence matches the mapped control.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a compliance or VSOC dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Consider: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `complete_records`, `record_complete`, `record_completeness_pct`, `reported`, `reported_to_authority`, `reporting_pct`.
